summary: Fire‑filled set with Midnight Hour, Good Lovin' & tight transitions
blurb: The show shines with a scorching In The Midnight Hour and a powerful Good Lovin' that showcases Pigpen’s vocal fire. Jerry delivers crisp, inventive guitar tones while Phil anchors the groove; highlights include Deep Elem Blues, Truckin’ and the seamless Not Fade Away → Goin’ Down The Road Feeling Bad → Turn On Your Lovelight sequence.
review:
December 27, 1970 at Legion Stadium captured one of the Dead’s early‑70s highs. Pigpen was on fire all night – his banter, yodeling and soulful lead on Turn On Your Lovelight set a gritty blues tone that carried through standout numbers like In The Midnight Hour, Good Lovin' and Deep Elem Blues. Jerry’s guitar was “superb” with inventive riffs (even a cheeky Sly Stone reference), while Phil’s bass stayed rock‑solid and his vocal contributions added a surprising country flavor on Cumberland Blues. The band moved effortlessly from the acoustic‑electric swing of Truckin’ into the tight China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider segue, then launched into an exhilarating Not Fade Away → Goin’ Down The Road Feeling Bad → Turn On Your Lovelight chain that kept the crowd buzzing. Overall it’s a crisp, raw soundboard capture that lets every nuance of this fiery performance shine.

For listeners wanting the best audio experience, the Miller soundboard (gd70-12-27.sbd.miller.23530.sbeok.shnf) offers excellent clarity with just enough audience ambience to preserve the live feel. It’s a must‑listen for anyone craving that early‑’70s Dead energy.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Both recordings are described with positive language, repeatedly praising the set’s energy, Pigpen’s vocal fire and standout songs (Midnight Hour, Good Lovin', Deep Elem Blues, Not Fade Away > GDTRFB > Lovelight). No negative comments appear, indicating strong consensus of an exceptional performance. The consistent acclaim across two soundboard sources justifies a high positive sentiment adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard with crisp, raw audio and minimal hiss; captures the band’s dynamics and audience ambience better than the alternative.

key_highlights: Pigpen’s charismatic vocal fire on Good Lovin' and Turn On Your Lovelight
Jerry’s superb guitar tone and inventive riffs throughout the set
Seamless Not Fade Away → Goin’ Down The Road Feeling Bad → Turn On Your Lovelight sequence
Tight transitions like China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider and strong acoustic‑electric flow
song_highlights: Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad

band_performance.Jerry: Superb guitar tone, inventive riffs (including a Sly Stone nod) and enthusiastic backing vocals.
band_performance.Phil: Top‑form vocal contributions and solid bass foundation; shows his ‘Phil Country’ flair on Cumberland Blues.
band_performance.Bob: Pigpen’s charismatic stage banter, powerful vocal deliveries on Lovelight and blues numbers, plus memorable yodeling.

must_listen_sequences: Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad
